verdict.description: The critic's judgment ONLY, never the orchestrator's outcome. `not_run` is orchestrator-only and never emitted here.

blocking_findings.description: A reader would be misled about a fact. A dropped_story MUST carry a well-formed `source` receipt or the orchestrator downgrades it.

advisory_findings.description: True but weaker than it should be. Published, never gates.

where.description: A JSON path into the issue, e.g. watchlist.merck.

source.description: REQUIRED on a dropped_story — the receipt. Strict mode: the key is always present; null on every other kind.

title: ResearchSwarm critic verdict
description: The one JSON object Codex must emit. Passed to `codex exec --output-schema` to pin the shape at the model boundary; the orchestrator re-validates it anyway (a broken critic must never silently pass), so this is a guardrail, not the guarantee.
